memory_recovered: Queue reconciliation requires a separate repository-snapshot input stage that preserves source path and blob identity, limits discovery to append-only operational records, and reports unresolved commits and evidence paths rather than silently accepting them.

observed: The prior M-002 record explicitly left repository-aware discovery unresolved.
The committed input builder accepts only operations/queue-updates and operations/evidence JSON records.
The committed input builder preserves source paths and blob SHAs and performs no mutation.
inferred: Separating discovery from reconciliation reduces the chance that missing or foreign records are mistaken for valid queue state.
proposed: Use this builder before queue-reconciler.mjs in a future repository-aware command.
superseded: Manual assembly of reconciler input as the only supported path.
unresolved: The GitHub connector must still enumerate directory records and verify every referenced commit before constructing a complete repository snapshot.
The new test file is committed but was not executed by a runtime in this run.

verification: The committed module was refetched by exact commit and its path allowlist, SHA validation, queue-item filtering, unresolved reference reporting, source inventory, privacy boundary, and mutation_performed=false fields were inspected. Runtime test execution was not available.
privacy_boundary: Only public repository operations metadata and implementation files were used. Raw chat, medical or pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
falsification_route: Provide a safe-root record with a malformed blob SHA that is accepted, a foreign queue item that enters updates, a missing reference marked resolvable, or any source mutation; any such result falsifies the contract.
